20	// register macros
21	`include "common_cells/registers.svh"
22	
23	module axi_lite_mux #(
24	  // AXI4-Lite parameter and channel types
25	  parameter type          aw_chan_t  = logic, // AW LITE Channel Type
26	  parameter type           w_chan_t  = logic, //  W LITE Channel Type
27	  parameter type           b_chan_t  = logic, //  B LITE Channel Type
28	  parameter type          ar_chan_t  = logic, // AR LITE Channel Type
29	  parameter type           r_chan_t  = logic, //  R LITE Channel Type
30	  parameter type          axi_req_t  = logic, // AXI4-Lite request type
31	  parameter type         axi_resp_t  = logic, // AXI4-Lite response type
32	  parameter int unsigned NoSlvPorts  = 32'd0, // Number of slave ports
33	  // Maximum number of outstanding transactions per write or read
34	  parameter int unsigned MaxTrans    = 32'd0,
35	  // If enabled, this multiplexer is purely combinatorial
36	  parameter bit          FallThrough = 1'b0,
37	  // add spill register on write master port, adds a cycle latency on write channels
38	  parameter bit          SpillAw     = 1'b1,
39	  parameter bit          SpillW      = 1'b0,
40	  parameter bit          SpillB      = 1'b0,
41	  // add spill register on read master port, adds a cycle latency on read channels
42	  parameter bit          SpillAr     = 1'b1,
43	  parameter bit          SpillR      = 1'b0
44	) (
45	  input  logic                       clk_i,    // Clock
46	  input  logic                       rst_ni,   // Asynchronous reset active low
47	  input  logic                       test_i,   // Test Mode enable
48	  // slave ports (AXI4-Lite inputs), connect master modules here
49	  input  axi_req_t  [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_reqs_i,
50	  output axi_resp_t [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_resps_o,
51	  // master port (AXI4-Lite output), connect slave module here
52	  output axi_req_t                   mst_req_o,
53	  input  axi_resp_t                  mst_resp_i
54	);
55	  // pass through if only one slave port
56	  if (NoSlvPorts == 32'h1) begin : gen_no_mux
57	    spill_register #(
58	      .T       ( aw_chan_t  ),
59	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAw   )
60	    ) i_aw_spill_reg (
61	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                    ),
62	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                   ),
63	      .valid_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].aw_valid   ),
64	      .ready_o ( slv_resps_o[0].aw_ready  ),
65	      .data_i  ( slv_reqs_i[0].aw         ),
66	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.aw_valid       ),
67	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.aw_ready      ),
68	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.aw             )
69	    );
70	    spill_register #(
71	      .T       ( w_chan_t ),
72	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillW  )
73	    ) i_w_spill_reg (
74	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                   ),
75	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                  ),
76	      .valid_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].w_valid   ),
77	      .ready_o ( slv_resps_o[0].w_ready  ),
78	      .data_i  ( slv_reqs_i[0].w         ),
79	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.w_valid       ),
80	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.w_ready      ),
81	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.w             )
82	    );
83	    spill_register #(
84	      .T       ( b_chan_t ),
85	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillB  )
86	    ) i_b_spill_reg (
87	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                  ),
88	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                 ),
89	      .valid_i ( mst_resp_i.b_valid     ),
90	      .ready_o ( mst_req_o.b_ready      ),
91	      .data_i  ( mst_resp_i.b           ),
92	      .valid_o ( slv_resps_o[0].b_valid ),
93	      .ready_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].b_ready  ),
94	      .data_o  ( slv_resps_o[0].b       )
95	    );
96	    spill_register #(
97	      .T       ( ar_chan_t ),
98	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAr  )
99	    ) i_ar_spill_reg (
100	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                    ),
101	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                   ),
102	      .valid_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].ar_valid   ),
103	      .ready_o ( slv_resps_o[0].ar_ready  ),
104	      .data_i  ( slv_reqs_i[0].ar         ),
105	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.ar_valid       ),
106	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.ar_ready      ),
107	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.ar             )
108	    );
109	    spill_register #(
110	      .T       ( r_chan_t ),
111	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillR  )
112	    ) i_r_spill_reg (
113	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                  ),
114	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                 ),
115	      .valid_i ( mst_resp_i.r_valid     ),
116	      .ready_o ( mst_req_o.r_ready      ),
117	      .data_i  ( mst_resp_i.r           ),
118	      .valid_o ( slv_resps_o[0].r_valid ),
119	      .ready_i ( slv_reqs_i[0].r_ready  ),
120	      .data_o  ( slv_resps_o[0].r       )
121	    );
122	
123	  // other non degenerate cases
124	  end else begin : gen_mux
125	    // typedef for the FIFO types
126	    // @rmcnulty: workaround for Verilator internal error
127	    localparam int unsigned CLog2NoSlvPorts = $clog2(NoSlvPorts);
128	    typedef logic [CLog2NoSlvPorts-1:0] select_t;
129	
130	    // input to the AW arbitration tree, unpacked from request struct
131	    aw_chan_t [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_aw_chans;
132	    logic     [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_aw_valids, slv_aw_readies;
133	
134	    // AW channel arb tree decision
135	    select_t  aw_select;
136	    aw_chan_t mst_aw_chan;
137	    logic     mst_aw_valid, mst_aw_ready;
138	
139	    // AW master handshake internal, so that we are able to stall, if w_fifo is full
140	    logic     aw_valid,     aw_ready;
141	
142	    // FF to lock the AW valid signal, when a new arbitration decision is made the decision
143	    // gets pushed into the W FIFO, when it now stalls prevent subsequent pushing
144	    // This FF removes AW to W dependency
145	    logic     lock_aw_valid_d, lock_aw_valid_q;
146	    logic     load_aw_lock;
147	
148	    // signals for the FIFO that holds the last switching decision of the AW channel
149	    logic     w_fifo_full,  w_fifo_empty;
150	    logic     w_fifo_push,  w_fifo_pop;
151	
152	    // W channel spill reg
153	    select_t  w_select;
154	    w_chan_t  mst_w_chan;
155	    logic     mst_w_valid,  mst_w_ready;
156	
157	    // switching decision for the B response back routing
158	    select_t  b_select;
159	    // signals for the FIFO that holds the last switching decision of the AW channel
160	    logic     b_fifo_full,  b_fifo_empty;
161	    logic     /*w_fifo_pop*/b_fifo_pop;
162	
163	    // B channel spill reg
164	    b_chan_t  mst_b_chan;
165	    logic     mst_b_valid,  mst_b_ready;
166	
167	    // input to the AR arbitration tree, unpacked from request struct
168	    ar_chan_t [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_ar_chans;
169	    logic     [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_ar_valids, slv_ar_readies;
170	
171	    // AR channel for when spill is enabled
172	    select_t  ar_select;
173	    ar_chan_t mst_ar_chan;
174	    logic     mst_ar_valid, mst_ar_ready;
175	
176	    // AR master handshake internal, so that we are able to stall, if R_fifo is full
177	    logic     ar_valid,     ar_ready;
178	
179	    // master ID in the r_id
180	    select_t  r_select;
181	
182	    // signals for the FIFO that holds the last switching decision of the AR channel
183	    logic     r_fifo_full,  r_fifo_empty;
184	    logic     r_fifo_push,  r_fifo_pop;
185	
186	    // R channel spill reg
187	    r_chan_t  mst_r_chan;
188	    logic     mst_r_valid,  mst_r_ready;
189	
190	    //--------------------------------------
191	    // AW Channel
192	    //--------------------------------------
193	    // unpach AW channel from request/response array
194	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_aw_arb_input
195	      assign slv_aw_chans[i]         = slv_reqs_i[i].aw;
196	      assign slv_aw_valids[i]        = slv_reqs_i[i].aw_valid;
197	      assign slv_resps_o[i].aw_ready = slv_aw_readies[i];
198	    end
199	    rr_arb_tree #(
200	      .NumIn    ( NoSlvPorts ),
201	      .DataType ( aw_chan_t  ),
202	      .AxiVldRdy( 1'b1       ),
203	      .LockIn   ( 1'b1       )
204	    ) i_aw_arbiter (
205	      .clk_i  ( clk_i          ),
206	      .rst_ni ( rst_ni         ),
207	      .flush_i( 1'b0           ),
208	      .rr_i   ( '0             ),
209	      .req_i  ( slv_aw_valids  ),
210	      .gnt_o  ( slv_aw_readies ),
211	      .data_i ( slv_aw_chans   ),
212	      .gnt_i  ( aw_ready       ),
213	      .req_o  ( aw_valid       ),
214	      .data_o ( mst_aw_chan    ),
215	      .idx_o  ( aw_select      )
216	    );
217	
218	    // control of the AW channel
219	    always_comb begin
220	      // default assignments
221	      lock_aw_valid_d = lock_aw_valid_q;
222	      load_aw_lock    = 1'b0;
223	      w_fifo_push     = 1'b0;
224	      mst_aw_valid    = 1'b0;
225	      aw_ready        = 1'b0;
226	      // had a downstream stall, be valid and send the AW along
227	      if (lock_aw_valid_q) begin
228	        mst_aw_valid = 1'b1;
229	        // transaction
230	        if (mst_aw_ready) begin
231	          aw_ready        = 1'b1;
232	          lock_aw_valid_d = 1'b0;
233	          load_aw_lock    = 1'b1;
234	        end
235	      end else begin
236	        if (!w_fifo_full && aw_valid) begin
237	          mst_aw_valid = 1'b1;
238	          w_fifo_push = 1'b1;
239	          if (mst_aw_ready) begin
240	            aw_ready = 1'b1;
241	          end else begin
242	            // go to lock if transaction not in this cycle
243	            lock_aw_valid_d = 1'b1;
244	            load_aw_lock    = 1'b1;
245	          end
246	        end
247	      end
248	    end
249	
250	    `FFLARN(lock_aw_valid_q, lock_aw_valid_d, load_aw_lock, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
251	
252	    fifo_v3 #(
253	      .FALL_THROUGH ( FallThrough ),
254	      .DEPTH        ( MaxTrans    ),
255	      .dtype        ( select_t    )
256	    ) i_w_fifo (
257	      .clk_i     ( clk_i        ),
258	      .rst_ni    ( rst_ni       ),
259	      .flush_i   ( 1'b0         ),
260	      .testmode_i( test_i       ),
261	      .full_o    ( w_fifo_full  ),
262	      .empty_o   ( w_fifo_empty ),
263	      .usage_o   (              ),
264	      .data_i    ( aw_select    ),
265	      .push_i    ( w_fifo_push  ),
266	      .data_o    ( w_select     ),
267	      .pop_i     ( w_fifo_pop   )
268	    );
269	
270	    spill_register #(
271	      .T       ( aw_chan_t  ),
272	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAw   ) // Param indicated that we want a spill reg
273	    ) i_aw_spill_reg (
274	      .clk_i   ( clk_i               ),
275	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni              ),
276	      .valid_i ( mst_aw_valid        ),
277	      .ready_o ( mst_aw_ready        ),
278	      .data_i  ( mst_aw_chan         ),
279	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.aw_valid  ),
280	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.aw_ready ),
281	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.aw        )
282	    );
283	
284	    //--------------------------------------
285	    // W Channel
286	    //--------------------------------------
287	    // multiplexer
288	    assign mst_w_chan      = slv_reqs_i[w_select].w;
289	    assign mst_w_valid     = (!w_fifo_empty && !b_fifo_full) ? slv_reqs_i[w_select].w_valid  : 1'b0;
290	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_slv_w_ready
291	      assign slv_resps_o[i].w_ready =  mst_w_ready & ~w_fifo_empty &
292	                                      ~b_fifo_full & (w_select == select_t'(i));
293	    end
294	    assign w_fifo_pop      = mst_w_valid & mst_w_ready;
295	
296	    fifo_v3 #(
297	      .FALL_THROUGH ( FallThrough ),
298	      .DEPTH        ( MaxTrans    ),
299	      .dtype        ( select_t    )
300	    ) i_b_fifo (
301	      .clk_i     ( clk_i        ),
302	      .rst_ni    ( rst_ni       ),
303	      .flush_i   ( 1'b0         ),
304	      .testmode_i( test_i       ),
305	      .full_o    ( b_fifo_full  ),
306	      .empty_o   ( b_fifo_empty ),
307	      .usage_o   (              ),
308	      .data_i    ( w_select     ),
309	      .push_i    ( w_fifo_pop   ), // push the selection for the B channel on W transaction
310	      .data_o    ( b_select     ),
311	      .pop_i     ( b_fifo_pop   )
312	    );
313	
314	    spill_register #(
315	      .T       ( w_chan_t ),
316	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillW  )
317	    ) i_w_spill_reg (
318	      .clk_i   ( clk_i              ),
319	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni             ),
320	      .valid_i ( mst_w_valid        ),
321	      .ready_o ( mst_w_ready        ),
322	      .data_i  ( mst_w_chan         ),
323	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.w_valid  ),
324	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.w_ready ),
325	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.w        )
326	    );
327	
328	    //--------------------------------------
329	    // B Channel
330	    //--------------------------------------
331	    // replicate B channels
332	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_slv_resps_b
333	      assign slv_resps_o[i].b       = mst_b_chan;
334	      assign slv_resps_o[i].b_valid = mst_b_valid & ~b_fifo_empty & (b_select == select_t'(i));
335	    end
336	    assign mst_b_ready    = ~b_fifo_empty & slv_reqs_i[b_select].b_ready;
337	    assign b_fifo_pop     = mst_b_valid & mst_b_ready;
338	
339	    spill_register #(
340	      .T       ( b_chan_t ),
341	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillB  )
342	    ) i_b_spill_reg (
343	      .clk_i   ( clk_i              ),
344	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni             ),
345	      .valid_i ( mst_resp_i.b_valid ),
346	      .ready_o ( mst_req_o.b_ready  ),
347	      .data_i  ( mst_resp_i.b       ),
348	      .valid_o ( mst_b_valid        ),
349	      .ready_i ( mst_b_ready        ),
350	      .data_o  ( mst_b_chan         )
351	    );
352	
353	    //--------------------------------------
354	    // AR Channel
355	    //--------------------------------------
356	    // unpack AR channel from request/response struct
357	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_ar_arb_input
358	      assign slv_ar_chans[i]         = slv_reqs_i[i].ar;
359	      assign slv_ar_valids[i]        = slv_reqs_i[i].ar_valid;
360	      assign slv_resps_o[i].ar_ready = slv_ar_readies[i];
361	    end
362	    rr_arb_tree #(
363	      .NumIn    ( NoSlvPorts ),
364	      .DataType ( ar_chan_t  ),
365	      .AxiVldRdy( 1'b1       ),
366	      .LockIn   ( 1'b1       )
367	    ) i_ar_arbiter (
368	      .clk_i  ( clk_i          ),
369	      .rst_ni ( rst_ni         ),
370	      .flush_i( 1'b0           ),
371	      .rr_i   ( '0             ),
372	      .req_i  ( slv_ar_valids  ),
373	      .gnt_o  ( slv_ar_readies ),
374	      .data_i ( slv_ar_chans   ),
375	      .gnt_i  ( ar_ready       ),
376	      .req_o  ( ar_valid       ),
377	      .data_o ( mst_ar_chan    ),
378	      .idx_o  ( ar_select      )
379	    );
380	
381	    // connect the handshake if there is space in the FIFO, no need for valid locking
382	    // as the R response is only allowed, when AR is transferred
383	    assign mst_ar_valid = (!r_fifo_full) ? ar_valid     : 1'b0;
384	    assign ar_ready     = (!r_fifo_full) ? mst_ar_ready : 1'b0;
385	    assign r_fifo_push  = mst_ar_valid & mst_ar_ready;
386	
387	    fifo_v3 #(
388	      .FALL_THROUGH ( FallThrough ),
389	      .DEPTH        ( MaxTrans    ),
390	      .dtype        ( select_t    )
391	    ) i_r_fifo (
392	      .clk_i     ( clk_i        ),
393	      .rst_ni    ( rst_ni       ),
394	      .flush_i   ( 1'b0         ),
395	      .testmode_i( test_i       ),
396	      .full_o    ( r_fifo_full  ),
397	      .empty_o   ( r_fifo_empty ),
398	      .usage_o   (              ),
399	      .data_i    ( ar_select    ),
400	      .push_i    ( r_fifo_push  ), // push the selection when w transaction happens
401	      .data_o    ( r_select     ),
402	      .pop_i     ( r_fifo_pop   )
403	    );
404	
405	    spill_register #(
406	      .T       ( ar_chan_t ),
407	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAr  )
408	    ) i_ar_spill_reg (
409	      .clk_i   ( clk_i               ),
410	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni              ),
411	      .valid_i ( mst_ar_valid        ),
412	      .ready_o ( mst_ar_ready        ),
413	      .data_i  ( mst_ar_chan         ),
414	      .valid_o ( mst_req_o.ar_valid  ),
415	      .ready_i ( mst_resp_i.ar_ready ),
416	      .data_o  ( mst_req_o.ar        )
417	    );
418	
419	    //--------------------------------------
420	    // R Channel
421	    //--------------------------------------
422	    // replicate R channels
423	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_slv_resps_r
424	      assign slv_resps_o[i].r       = mst_r_chan;
425	      assign slv_resps_o[i].r_valid = mst_r_valid & ~r_fifo_empty & (r_select == select_t'(i));
426	    end
427	    assign mst_r_ready    = ~r_fifo_empty & slv_reqs_i[r_select].r_ready;
428	    assign r_fifo_pop     = mst_r_valid & mst_r_ready;
429	
430	    spill_register #(
431	      .T       ( r_chan_t ),
432	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillR  )
433	    ) i_r_spill_reg (
434	      .clk_i   ( clk_i              ),
435	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni             ),
436	      .valid_i ( mst_resp_i.r_valid ),
437	      .ready_o ( mst_req_o.r_ready  ),
438	      .data_i  ( mst_resp_i.r       ),
439	      .valid_o ( mst_r_valid        ),
440	      .ready_i ( mst_r_ready        ),
441	      .data_o  ( mst_r_chan         )
442	    );
443	  end
444	
445	  // pragma translate_off
446	  `ifndef VERILATOR
447	    initial begin: p_assertions
448	      NoPorts:  assert (NoSlvPorts > 0) else $fatal(1, "Number of slave ports must be at least 1!");
449	      MaxTnx:   assert (MaxTrans   > 0) else $fatal(1, "Number of transactions must be at least 1!");
450	    end
451	  `endif
452	  // pragma translate_on
453	endmodule
454	
455	// interface wrap
456	`include "axi/assign.svh"
457	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
458	
459	module axi_lite_mux_intf #(
460	  parameter int unsigned AxiAddrWidth  = 32'd0,
461	  parameter int unsigned AxiDataWidth  = 32'd0,
462	  parameter int unsigned NoSlvPorts    = 32'd0, // Number of slave ports
463	  // Maximum number of outstanding transactions per write
464	  parameter int unsigned MaxTrans      = 32'd0,
465	  // if enabled, this multiplexer is purely combinatorial
466	  parameter bit          FallThrough   = 1'b0,
467	  // add spill register on write master ports, adds a cycle latency on write channels
468	  parameter bit          SpillAw       = 1'b1,
469	  parameter bit          SpillW        = 1'b0,
470	  parameter bit          SpillB        = 1'b0,
471	  // add spill register on read master ports, adds a cycle latency on read channels
472	  parameter bit          SpillAr       = 1'b1,
473	  parameter bit          SpillR        = 1'b0
474	) (
475	  input  logic    clk_i,                // Clock
476	  input  logic    rst_ni,               // Asynchronous reset active low
477	  input  logic    test_i,               // Testmode enable
478	  AXI_LITE.Slave  slv [NoSlvPorts-1:0], // slave ports
479	  AXI_LITE.Master mst                   // master port
480	);
481	
482	  typedef logic [AxiAddrWidth-1:0]   addr_t;
483	  typedef logic [AxiDataWidth-1:0]   data_t;
484	  typedef logic [AxiDataWidth/8-1:0] strb_t;
485	  // channels typedef
486	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(aw_chan_t, addr_t)
487	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(w_chan_t, data_t, strb_t)
488	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(b_chan_t)
489	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(ar_chan_t, addr_t)
490	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(r_chan_t, data_t)
491	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(axi_req_t, aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, ar_chan_t)
492	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(axi_resp_t, b_chan_t, r_chan_t)
493	
494	  axi_req_t     [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_reqs;
495	  axi_resp_t    [NoSlvPorts-1:0] slv_resps;
496	  axi_req_t                      mst_req;
497	  axi_resp_t                     mst_resp;
498	
499	  for (genvar i = 0; i < NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_assign_slv_ports
500	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(slv_reqs[i], slv[i])
501	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv[i], slv_resps[i])
502	  end
503	
504	  `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_FROM_REQ(mst, mst_req)
505	  `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_TO_RESP(mst_resp, mst)
506	
507	  axi_lite_mux #(
508	    .aw_chan_t     ( aw_chan_t     ), // AW Channel Type
509	    .w_chan_t      (  w_chan_t     ), //  W Channel Type
510	    .b_chan_t      (  b_chan_t     ), //  B Channel Type
511	    .ar_chan_t     ( ar_chan_t     ), // AR Channel Type
512	    .r_chan_t      (  r_chan_t     ), //  R Channel Type
513	    .axi_req_t     ( axi_req_t     ),
514	    .axi_resp_t    ( axi_resp_t    ),
515	    .NoSlvPorts    ( NoSlvPorts    ), // Number of slave ports
516	    .MaxTrans      ( MaxTrans      ),
517	    .FallThrough   ( FallThrough   ),
518	    .SpillAw       ( SpillAw       ),
519	    .SpillW        ( SpillW        ),
520	    .SpillB        ( SpillB        ),
521	    .SpillAr       ( SpillAr       ),
522	    .SpillR        ( SpillR        )
523	  ) i_axi_mux (
524	    .clk_i,  // Clock
525	    .rst_ni, // Asynchronous reset active low
526	    .test_i, // Test Mode enable
527	    .slv_reqs_i  ( slv_reqs  ),
528	    .slv_resps_o ( slv_resps ),
529	    .mst_req_o   ( mst_req   ),
530	    .mst_resp_i  ( mst_resp  )
531	  );
532	
533	  // pragma translate_off
534	  `ifndef VERILATOR
535	    initial begin: p_assertions
536	      AddrWidth: assert (AxiAddrWidth > 0) else $fatal(1, "Axi Parameter has to be > 0!");
537	      DataWidth: assert (AxiDataWidth > 0) else $fatal(1, "Axi Parameter has to be > 0!");
538	    end
539	  `endif
540	  // pragma translate_on
541	endmodule
542

15	`include "common_cells/registers.svh"
16	
17	`ifdef QUESTA
18	// Derive `TARGET_VSIM`, which is used for tool-specific workarounds in this file, from `QUESTA`,
19	// which is automatically set in Questa.
20	`define TARGET_VSIM
21	`endif
22	
23	// axi_lite_demux: Demultiplex an AXI4-Lite bus from one slave port to multiple master ports.
24	//                 The selection signal at the AW and AR channel has to follow the same
25	//                 stability rules as the corresponding AXI4-Lite channel.
26	
27	module axi_lite_demux #(
28	  parameter type         aw_chan_t      = logic, // AXI4-Lite AW channel
29	  parameter type         w_chan_t       = logic, // AXI4-Lite  W channel
30	  parameter type         b_chan_t       = logic, // AXI4-Lite  B channel
31	  parameter type         ar_chan_t      = logic, // AXI4-Lite AR channel
32	  parameter type         r_chan_t       = logic, // AXI4-Lite  R channel
33	  parameter type         axi_req_t      = logic, // AXI4-Lite request struct
34	  parameter type         axi_resp_t     = logic, // AXI4-Lite response struct
35	  parameter int unsigned NoMstPorts     = 32'd0, // Number of instantiated ports
36	  parameter int unsigned MaxTrans       = 32'd0, // Maximum number of open transactions per channel
37	  parameter bit          FallThrough    = 1'b0,  // FIFOs are in fall through mode
38	  parameter bit          SpillAw        = 1'b1,  // insert one cycle latency on slave AW
39	  parameter bit          SpillW         = 1'b0,  // insert one cycle latency on slave  W
40	  parameter bit          SpillB         = 1'b0,  // insert one cycle latency on slave  B
41	  parameter bit          SpillAr        = 1'b1,  // insert one cycle latency on slave AR
42	  parameter bit          SpillR         = 1'b0,  // insert one cycle latency on slave  R
43	  // Dependent parameters, DO NOT OVERRIDE!
44	  parameter type         select_t       = logic [$clog2(NoMstPorts)-1:0]
45	) (
46	  input  logic                        clk_i,
47	  input  logic                        rst_ni,
48	  input  logic                        test_i,
49	  // slave port (AXI4-Lite input), connect master module here
50	  input  axi_req_t                    slv_req_i,
51	  input  select_t                     slv_aw_select_i,
52	  input  select_t                     slv_ar_select_i,
53	  output axi_resp_t                   slv_resp_o,
54	  // master ports (AXI4-Lite outputs), connect slave modules here
55	  output axi_req_t  [NoMstPorts-1:0]  mst_reqs_o,
56	  input  axi_resp_t [NoMstPorts-1:0]  mst_resps_i
57	);
58	
59	  //--------------------------------------
60	  // Typedefs for the spill registers
61	  //--------------------------------------
62	  typedef struct packed {
63	    aw_chan_t aw;
64	    select_t  select;
65	  } aw_chan_select_t;
66	  typedef struct packed {
67	    ar_chan_t ar;
68	    select_t  select;
69	  } ar_chan_select_t;
70	
71	  if (NoMstPorts == 32'd1) begin : gen_no_demux
72	    // degenerate case, connect slave to master port
73	    spill_register #(
74	      .T       ( aw_chan_t  ),
75	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAw   )
76	    ) i_aw_spill_reg (
77	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                    ),
78	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                   ),
79	      .valid_i ( slv_req_i.aw_valid       ),
80	      .ready_o ( slv_resp_o.aw_ready      ),
81	      .data_i  ( slv_req_i.aw             ),
82	      .valid_o ( mst_reqs_o[0].aw_valid   ),
83	      .ready_i ( mst_resps_i[0].aw_ready  ),
84	      .data_o  ( mst_reqs_o[0].aw         )
85	    );
86	    spill_register #(
87	      .T       ( w_chan_t  ),
88	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillW   )
89	    ) i_w_spill_reg (
90	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                   ),
91	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                  ),
92	      .valid_i ( slv_req_i.w_valid       ),
93	      .ready_o ( slv_resp_o.w_ready      ),
94	      .data_i  ( slv_req_i.w             ),
95	      .valid_o ( mst_reqs_o[0].w_valid   ),
96	      .ready_i ( mst_resps_i[0].w_ready  ),
97	      .data_o  ( mst_reqs_o[0].w         )
98	    );
99	    spill_register #(
100	      .T       ( b_chan_t ),
101	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillB      )
102	    ) i_b_spill_reg (
103	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                  ),
104	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                 ),
105	      .valid_i ( mst_resps_i[0].b_valid ),
106	      .ready_o ( mst_reqs_o[0].b_ready  ),
107	      .data_i  ( mst_resps_i[0].b       ),
108	      .valid_o ( slv_resp_o.b_valid     ),
109	      .ready_i ( slv_req_i.b_ready      ),
110	      .data_o  ( slv_resp_o.b           )
111	    );
112	    spill_register #(
113	      .T       ( ar_chan_t  ),
114	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillAr   )
115	    ) i_ar_spill_reg (
116	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                    ),
117	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                   ),
118	      .valid_i ( slv_req_i.ar_valid       ),
119	      .ready_o ( slv_resp_o.ar_ready      ),
120	      .data_i  ( slv_req_i.ar             ),
121	      .valid_o ( mst_reqs_o[0].ar_valid   ),
122	      .ready_i ( mst_resps_i[0].ar_ready  ),
123	      .data_o  ( mst_reqs_o[0].ar         )
124	    );
125	    spill_register #(
126	      .T       ( r_chan_t ),
127	      .Bypass  ( ~SpillR      )
128	    ) i_r_spill_reg (
129	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                  ),
130	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                 ),
131	      .valid_i ( mst_resps_i[0].r_valid ),
132	      .ready_o ( mst_reqs_o[0].r_ready  ),
133	      .data_i  ( mst_resps_i[0].r       ),
134	      .valid_o ( slv_resp_o.r_valid     ),
135	      .ready_i ( slv_req_i.r_ready      ),
136	      .data_o  ( slv_resp_o.r           )
137	    );
138	
139	  end else begin : gen_demux
140	    // normal non degenerate case
141	
142	
143	    //--------------------------------------
144	    //--------------------------------------
145	    // Signal Declarations
146	    //--------------------------------------
147	    //--------------------------------------
148	
149	    //--------------------------------------
150	    // Write Transaction
151	    //--------------------------------------
152	    aw_chan_select_t       slv_aw_chan;
153	    logic                  slv_aw_valid,    slv_aw_ready;
154	
155	    logic [NoMstPorts-1:0] mst_aw_valids, mst_aw_readies;
156	
157	    logic                  lock_aw_valid_d, lock_aw_valid_q, load_aw_lock;
158	
159	    logic                  w_fifo_push,     w_fifo_pop;
160	    logic                  w_fifo_full,     w_fifo_empty;
161	
162	    w_chan_t               slv_w_chan;
163	    select_t               w_select;
164	    logic                  slv_w_valid,     slv_w_ready;
165	
166	    logic                  /*w_pop*/        b_fifo_pop;
167	    logic                  b_fifo_full,     b_fifo_empty;
168	
169	    b_chan_t               slv_b_chan;
170	    select_t               b_select;
171	    logic                  slv_b_valid,     slv_b_ready;
172	
173	    //--------------------------------------
174	    // Read Transaction
175	    //--------------------------------------
176	    ar_chan_select_t slv_ar_chan;
177	    logic            slv_ar_valid,    slv_ar_ready;
178	
179	    logic            r_fifo_push,     r_fifo_pop;
180	    logic            r_fifo_full,     r_fifo_empty;
181	
182	    r_chan_t         slv_r_chan;
183	    select_t         r_select;
184	    logic            slv_r_valid,     slv_r_ready;
185	
186	    //--------------------------------------
187	    //--------------------------------------
188	    // Channel control
189	    //--------------------------------------
190	    //--------------------------------------
191	
192	    //--------------------------------------
193	    // AW Channel
194	    //--------------------------------------
195	    `ifdef TARGET_VSIM
196	    // Workaround for bug in Questa 2020.2 and 2021.1: Flatten the struct into a logic vector before
197	    // instantiating `spill_register`.
198	    typedef logic [$bits(aw_chan_select_t)-1:0] aw_chan_select_flat_t;
199	    `else
200	    // Other tools, such as VCS, have problems with `$bits()`, so the workaround cannot be used
201	    // generally.
202	    typedef aw_chan_select_t aw_chan_select_flat_t;
203	    `endif
204	    aw_chan_select_flat_t slv_aw_chan_select_in_flat,
205	                          slv_aw_chan_select_out_flat;
206	    assign slv_aw_chan_select_in_flat = {slv_req_i.aw, slv_aw_select_i};
207	    spill_register #(
208	      .T      ( aw_chan_select_flat_t         ),
209	      .Bypass ( ~SpillAw                      )
210	    ) i_aw_spill_reg (
211	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                        ),
212	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                       ),
213	      .valid_i ( slv_req_i.aw_valid           ),
214	      .ready_o ( slv_resp_o.aw_ready          ),
215	      .data_i  ( slv_aw_chan_select_in_flat   ),
216	      .valid_o ( slv_aw_valid                 ),
217	      .ready_i ( slv_aw_ready                 ),
218	      .data_o  ( slv_aw_chan_select_out_flat  )
219	    );
220	    assign slv_aw_chan = slv_aw_chan_select_out_flat;
221	
222	    // replicate AW channel to the request output
223	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_mst_aw
224	      assign mst_reqs_o[i].aw       = slv_aw_chan.aw;
225	      assign mst_reqs_o[i].aw_valid = mst_aw_valids[i];
226	      assign mst_aw_readies[i]      = mst_resps_i[i].aw_ready;
227	    end
228	
229	    // AW channel handshake control
230	    always_comb begin
231	      // default assignments
232	      lock_aw_valid_d = lock_aw_valid_q;
233	      load_aw_lock    = 1'b0;
234	      // handshake
235	      slv_aw_ready    = 1'b0;
236	      mst_aw_valids   = '0;
237	      // W FIFO input control
238	      w_fifo_push     = 1'b0;
239	      // control
240	      if (lock_aw_valid_q) begin
241	        // AW channel is locked and has valid output, fifo was pushed, as the new request was issued
242	        mst_aw_valids[slv_aw_chan.select] = 1'b1;
243	        if (mst_aw_readies[slv_aw_chan.select]) begin
244	          // transaction, go back to IDLE
245	          slv_aw_ready    = 1'b1;
246	          lock_aw_valid_d = 1'b0;
247	          load_aw_lock    = 1'b1;
248	        end
249	      end else begin
250	        if (!w_fifo_full && slv_aw_valid) begin
251	          // new transaction, push select in the FIFO and then look if transaction happened
252	          w_fifo_push                       = 1'b1;
253	          mst_aw_valids[slv_aw_chan.select] = 1'b1; // only set the valid when FIFO is not full
254	          if (mst_aw_readies[slv_aw_chan.select]) begin
255	            // transaction, notify slave port
256	            slv_aw_ready = 1'b1;
257	          end else begin
258	            // no transaction, lock valid
259	            lock_aw_valid_d = 1'b1;
260	            load_aw_lock    = 1'b1;
261	          end
262	        end
263	      end
264	    end
265	
266	    // lock the valid signal, as the selection gets pushed into the W FIFO on first assertion,
267	    // prevent further pushing
268	    `FFLARN(lock_aw_valid_q, lock_aw_valid_d, load_aw_lock, '0, clk_i, rst_ni)
269	
270	    fifo_v3 #(
271	      .FALL_THROUGH( FallThrough ),
272	      .DEPTH       ( MaxTrans    ),
273	      .dtype       ( select_t    )
274	    ) i_w_fifo (
275	      .clk_i      ( clk_i              ),
276	      .rst_ni     ( rst_ni             ),
277	      .flush_i    ( 1'b0               ), // not used, because AXI4-Lite no preemtion rule
278	      .testmode_i ( test_i             ),
279	      .full_o     ( w_fifo_full        ),
280	      .empty_o    ( w_fifo_empty       ),
281	      .usage_o    ( /*not used*/       ),
282	      .data_i     ( slv_aw_chan.select ),
283	      .push_i     ( w_fifo_push        ),
284	      .data_o     ( w_select           ),
285	      .pop_i      ( w_fifo_pop         )
286	    );
287	
288	    //--------------------------------------
289	    // W Channel
290	    //--------------------------------------
291	    spill_register #(
292	      .T      ( w_chan_t ),
293	      .Bypass ( ~SpillW  )
294	    ) i_w_spill_reg (
295	      .clk_i   ( clk_i              ),
296	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni             ),
297	      .valid_i ( slv_req_i.w_valid  ),
298	      .ready_o ( slv_resp_o.w_ready ),
299	      .data_i  ( slv_req_i.w        ),
300	      .valid_o ( slv_w_valid        ),
301	      .ready_i ( slv_w_ready        ),
302	      .data_o  ( slv_w_chan         )
303	    );
304	
305	    // replicate W channel
306	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_mst_w
307	      assign mst_reqs_o[i].w       = slv_w_chan;
308	      assign mst_reqs_o[i].w_valid = ~w_fifo_empty & ~b_fifo_full &
309	                                       slv_w_valid & (w_select == select_t'(i));
310	    end
311	    assign slv_w_ready = ~w_fifo_empty & ~b_fifo_full & mst_resps_i[w_select].w_ready;
312	    assign w_fifo_pop  = slv_w_valid & slv_w_ready;
313	
314	    fifo_v3 #(
315	      .FALL_THROUGH( FallThrough ),
316	      .DEPTH       ( MaxTrans    ),
317	      .dtype       ( select_t    )
318	    ) i_b_fifo (
319	      .clk_i      ( clk_i        ),
320	      .rst_ni     ( rst_ni       ),
321	      .flush_i    ( 1'b0         ), // not used, because AXI4-Lite no preemption
322	      .testmode_i ( test_i       ),
323	      .full_o     ( b_fifo_full  ),
324	      .empty_o    ( b_fifo_empty ),
325	      .usage_o    ( /*not used*/ ),
326	      .data_i     ( w_select     ),
327	      .push_i     ( w_fifo_pop   ), // w beat was transferred, push selection to b channel
328	      .data_o     ( b_select     ),
329	      .pop_i      ( b_fifo_pop   )
330	    );
331	
332	    //--------------------------------------
333	    // B Channel
334	    //--------------------------------------
335	    spill_register #(
336	      .T      ( b_chan_t ),
337	      .Bypass ( ~SpillB  )
338	    ) i_b_spill_reg (
339	      .clk_i   ( clk_i              ),
340	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni             ),
341	      .valid_i ( slv_b_valid        ),
342	      .ready_o ( slv_b_ready        ),
343	      .data_i  ( slv_b_chan         ),
344	      .valid_o ( slv_resp_o.b_valid ),
345	      .ready_i ( slv_req_i.b_ready  ),
346	      .data_o  ( slv_resp_o.b       )
347	    );
348	
349	    // connect the response if the FIFO has valid data in it
350	    assign slv_b_chan      = (!b_fifo_empty) ? mst_resps_i[b_select].b : '0;
351	    assign slv_b_valid     =  ~b_fifo_empty  & mst_resps_i[b_select].b_valid;
352	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_mst_b
353	      assign mst_reqs_o[i].b_ready = ~b_fifo_empty & slv_b_ready & (b_select == select_t'(i));
354	    end
355	    assign b_fifo_pop = slv_b_valid & slv_b_ready;
356	
357	    //--------------------------------------
358	    // AR Channel
359	    //--------------------------------------
360	    // Workaround for bug in Questa (see comments on AW channel for details).
361	    `ifdef TARGET_VSIM
362	    typedef logic [$bits(ar_chan_select_t)-1:0] ar_chan_select_flat_t;
363	    `else
364	    typedef ar_chan_select_t ar_chan_select_flat_t;
365	    `endif
366	    ar_chan_select_flat_t slv_ar_chan_select_in_flat,
367	                          slv_ar_chan_select_out_flat;
368	    assign slv_ar_chan_select_in_flat = {slv_req_i.ar, slv_ar_select_i};
369	    spill_register #(
370	      .T      ( ar_chan_select_flat_t         ),
371	      .Bypass ( ~SpillAr                      )
372	    ) i_ar_spill_reg (
373	      .clk_i   ( clk_i                        ),
374	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni                       ),
375	      .valid_i ( slv_req_i.ar_valid           ),
376	      .ready_o ( slv_resp_o.ar_ready          ),
377	      .data_i  ( slv_ar_chan_select_in_flat   ),
378	      .valid_o ( slv_ar_valid                 ),
379	      .ready_i ( slv_ar_ready                 ),
380	      .data_o  ( slv_ar_chan_select_out_flat  )
381	    );
382	    assign slv_ar_chan = slv_ar_chan_select_out_flat;
383	
384	    // replicate AR channel
385	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_mst_ar
386	      assign mst_reqs_o[i].ar       = slv_ar_chan.ar;
387	      assign mst_reqs_o[i].ar_valid = ~r_fifo_full & slv_ar_valid &
388	                                       (slv_ar_chan.select == select_t'(i));
389	    end
390	    assign slv_ar_ready = ~r_fifo_full & mst_resps_i[slv_ar_chan.select].ar_ready;
391	    assign r_fifo_push  = slv_ar_valid & slv_ar_ready;
392	
393	    fifo_v3 #(
394	      .FALL_THROUGH( FallThrough ),
395	      .DEPTH       ( MaxTrans    ),
396	      .dtype       ( select_t    )
397	    ) i_r_fifo (
398	      .clk_i      ( clk_i              ),
399	      .rst_ni     ( rst_ni             ),
400	      .flush_i    ( 1'b0               ), // not used, because AXI4-Lite no preemption rule
401	      .testmode_i ( test_i             ),
402	      .full_o     ( r_fifo_full        ),
403	      .empty_o    ( r_fifo_empty       ),
404	      .usage_o    ( /*not used*/       ),
405	      .data_i     ( slv_ar_chan.select ),
406	      .push_i     ( r_fifo_push        ),
407	      .data_o     ( r_select           ),
408	      .pop_i      ( r_fifo_pop         )
409	    );
410	
411	    //--------------------------------------
412	    // R Channel
413	    //--------------------------------------
414	    spill_register #(
415	      .T      ( r_chan_t ),
416	      .Bypass ( ~SpillR  )
417	    ) i_r_spill_reg (
418	      .clk_i   ( clk_i              ),
419	      .rst_ni  ( rst_ni             ),
420	      .valid_i ( slv_r_valid        ),
421	      .ready_o ( slv_r_ready        ),
422	      .data_i  ( slv_r_chan         ),
423	      .valid_o ( slv_resp_o.r_valid ),
424	      .ready_i ( slv_req_i.r_ready  ),
425	      .data_o  ( slv_resp_o.r       )
426	    );
427	
428	    // connect the response if the FIFO has valid data in it
429	    always_comb begin
430	       slv_r_chan  = '0;
431	       slv_r_valid = '0;
432	       if (!r_fifo_empty) begin
433	          slv_r_chan  = mst_resps_i[r_select].r;
434	          slv_r_valid = mst_resps_i[r_select].r_valid;
435	       end
436	    end
437	
438	    for (genvar i = 0; i < NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_mst_r
439	      assign mst_reqs_o[i].r_ready = ~r_fifo_empty & slv_r_ready & (r_select == select_t'(i));
440	    end
441	    assign r_fifo_pop      = slv_r_valid & slv_r_ready;
442	
443	    // pragma translate_off
444	    `ifndef VERILATOR
445	    `ifndef XSIM
446	    default disable iff (!rst_ni);
447	    aw_select: assume property( @(posedge clk_i) (slv_req_i.aw_valid |->
448	                                                 (slv_aw_select_i < NoMstPorts))) else
449	      $fatal(1, "slv_aw_select_i is %d: AW has selected a slave that is not defined.\
450	                 NoMstPorts: %d", slv_aw_select_i, NoMstPorts);
451	    ar_select: assume property( @(posedge clk_i) (slv_req_i.ar_valid |->
452	                                                 (slv_ar_select_i < NoMstPorts))) else
453	      $fatal(1, "slv_ar_select_i is %d: AR has selected a slave that is not defined.\
454	                 NoMstPorts: %d", slv_ar_select_i, NoMstPorts);
455	    aw_valid_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (slv_aw_valid && !slv_aw_ready)
456	                                                       |=> slv_aw_valid) else
457	      $fatal(1, "aw_valid was deasserted, when aw_ready = 0 in last cycle.");
458	    ar_valid_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (slv_ar_valid && !slv_ar_ready)
459	                                                       |=> slv_ar_valid) else
460	      $fatal(1, "ar_valid was deasserted, when ar_ready = 0 in last cycle.");
461	    aw_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (slv_aw_valid && !slv_aw_ready)
462	                               |=> $stable(slv_aw_chan)) else
463	      $fatal(1, "slv_aw_chan_select unstable with valid set.");
464	    ar_stable: assert property( @(posedge clk_i) (slv_ar_valid && !slv_ar_ready)
465	                               |=> $stable(slv_ar_chan)) else
466	      $fatal(1, "slv_aw_chan_select unstable with valid set.");
467	    `endif
468	    `endif
469	    // pragma translate_on
470	  end
471	
472	  // pragma translate_off
473	  `ifndef VERILATOR
474	    initial begin: p_assertions
475	      NoPorts:  assert (NoMstPorts > 0) else $fatal(1, "Number of master ports must be at least 1!");
476	      MaxTnx:   assert (MaxTrans   > 0) else $fatal(1, "Number of transactions must be at least 1!");
477	    end
478	  `endif
479	  // pragma translate_on
480	endmodule
481	
482	`include "axi/assign.svh"
483	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
484	
485	module axi_lite_demux_intf #(
486	  parameter int unsigned AxiAddrWidth = 32'd0,
487	  parameter int unsigned AxiDataWidth = 32'd0,
488	  parameter int unsigned NoMstPorts   = 32'd0,
489	  parameter int unsigned MaxTrans     = 32'd0,
490	  parameter bit          FallThrough  = 1'b0,
491	  parameter bit          SpillAw      = 1'b1,
492	  parameter bit          SpillW       = 1'b0,
493	  parameter bit          SpillB       = 1'b0,
494	  parameter bit          SpillAr      = 1'b1,
495	  parameter bit          SpillR       = 1'b0,
496	  // Dependent parameters, DO NOT OVERRIDE!
497	  parameter type         select_t     = logic [$clog2(NoMstPorts)-1:0]
498	) (
499	  input  logic     clk_i,               // Clock
500	  input  logic     rst_ni,              // Asynchronous reset active low
501	  input  logic     test_i,              // Testmode enable
502	  input  select_t  slv_aw_select_i,     // has to be stable, when aw_valid
503	  input  select_t  slv_ar_select_i,     // has to be stable, when ar_valid
504	  AXI_LITE.Slave   slv,                 // slave port
505	  AXI_LITE.Master  mst [NoMstPorts-1:0] // master ports
506	);
507	  typedef logic [AxiAddrWidth-1:0]   addr_t;
508	  typedef logic [AxiDataWidth-1:0]   data_t;
509	  typedef logic [AxiDataWidth/8-1:0] strb_t;
510	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(aw_chan_t, addr_t)
511	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(w_chan_t, data_t, strb_t)
512	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(b_chan_t)
513	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(ar_chan_t, addr_t)
514	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(r_chan_t, data_t)
515	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(axi_req_t, aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, ar_chan_t)
516	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(axi_resp_t, b_chan_t, r_chan_t)
517	
518	  axi_req_t                   slv_req;
519	  axi_resp_t                  slv_resp;
520	  axi_req_t  [NoMstPorts-1:0] mst_reqs;
521	  axi_resp_t [NoMstPorts-1:0] mst_resps;
522	
523	  `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(slv_req, slv)
524	  `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv, slv_resp)
525	
526	  for (genvar i = 0; i < NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_assign_mst_ports
527	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_FROM_REQ(mst[i], mst_reqs[i])
528	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_TO_RESP(mst_resps[i], mst[i])
529	  end
530	
531	  axi_lite_demux #(
532	    .aw_chan_t   (  aw_chan_t  ),
533	    .w_chan_t    (   w_chan_t  ),
534	    .b_chan_t    (   b_chan_t  ),
535	    .ar_chan_t   (  ar_chan_t  ),
536	    .r_chan_t    (   r_chan_t  ),
537	    .axi_req_t   (  axi_req_t  ),
538	    .axi_resp_t  ( axi_resp_t  ),
539	    .NoMstPorts  ( NoMstPorts  ),
540	    .MaxTrans    ( MaxTrans    ),
541	    .FallThrough ( FallThrough ),
542	    .SpillAw     ( SpillAw     ),
543	    .SpillW      ( SpillW      ),
544	    .SpillB      ( SpillB      ),
545	    .SpillAr     ( SpillAr     ),
546	    .SpillR      ( SpillR      )
547	  ) i_axi_demux (
548	    .clk_i,
549	    .rst_ni,
550	    .test_i,
551	    // slave Port
552	    .slv_req_i       ( slv_req         ),
553	    .slv_aw_select_i ( slv_aw_select_i ), // must be stable while slv_aw_valid_i
554	    .slv_ar_select_i ( slv_ar_select_i ), // must be stable while slv_ar_valid_i
555	    .slv_resp_o      ( slv_resp        ),
556	    // mster ports
557	    .mst_reqs_o      ( mst_reqs        ),
558	    .mst_resps_i     ( mst_resps       )
559	  );
560	
561	  // pragma translate_off
562	  `ifndef VERILATOR
563	    initial begin: p_assertions
564	      AddrWidth: assert (AxiAddrWidth > 0) else $fatal(1, "Axi Parmeter has to be > 0!");
565	      DataWidth: assert (AxiDataWidth > 0) else $fatal(1, "Axi Parmeter has to be > 0!");
566	    end
567	  `endif
568	  // pragma translate_on
569	endmodule

16	// axi_lite_xbar: Fully-connected AXI4-Lite crossbar.
17	// See `doc/axi_lite_xbar.md` for the documentation,
18	// including the definition of parameters and ports.
19	
20	`include "axi/typedef.svh"
21	
22	module axi_lite_xbar #(
23	  parameter axi_pkg::xbar_cfg_t Cfg = '0,
24	  parameter type  aw_chan_t         = logic,
25	  parameter type   w_chan_t         = logic,
26	  parameter type   b_chan_t         = logic,
27	  parameter type  ar_chan_t         = logic,
28	  parameter type   r_chan_t         = logic,
29	  parameter type  axi_req_t         = logic,
30	  parameter type axi_resp_t         = logic,
31	  parameter type     rule_t         = axi_pkg::xbar_rule_64_t,
32	  // DEPENDENT PARAMETERS, DO NOT OVERWRITE!
33	  parameter int unsigned MstIdxWidth = (Cfg.NoMstPorts > 32'd1) ? $clog2(Cfg.NoMstPorts) : 32'd1
34	) (
35	  input  logic                                        clk_i,
36	  input  logic                                        rst_ni,
37	  input  logic                                        test_i,
38	  input  axi_req_t  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]              slv_ports_req_i,
39	  output axi_resp_t [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]              slv_ports_resp_o,
40	  output axi_req_t  [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0]              mst_ports_req_o,
41	  input  axi_resp_t [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0]              mst_ports_resp_i,
42	  input  rule_t [Cfg.NoAddrRules-1:0]                 addr_map_i,
43	  input  logic  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]                  en_default_mst_port_i,
44	  input  logic  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0][MstIdxWidth-1:0] default_mst_port_i
45	);
46	
47	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiAddrWidth-1:0]   addr_t;
48	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiDataWidth-1:0]   data_t;
49	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiDataWidth/8-1:0] strb_t;
50	  // to account for the decoding error slave
51	  typedef logic [$clog2(Cfg.NoMstPorts + 1)-1:0] mst_port_idx_t;
52	  // full AXI typedef for the decode error slave, id_t and user_t are logic and will be
53	  // removed during logic optimization as they are stable
54	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(full_aw_chan_t, addr_t, logic, logic)
55	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(full_w_chan_t, data_t, strb_t, logic)
56	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(full_b_chan_t, logic, logic)
57	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(full_ar_chan_t, addr_t, logic, logic)
58	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(full_r_chan_t, data_t, logic, logic)
59	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(full_req_t, full_aw_chan_t, full_w_chan_t, full_ar_chan_t)
60	  `AXI_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(full_resp_t, full_b_chan_t, full_r_chan_t)
61	
62	  // signals from the axi_lite_demuxes, one index more for decode error routing
63	  axi_req_t  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0][Cfg.NoMstPorts:0] slv_reqs;
64	  axi_resp_t [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0][Cfg.NoMstPorts:0] slv_resps;
65	
66	  // signals into the axi_lite_muxes, are of type slave as the multiplexer extends the ID
67	  axi_req_t  [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0][Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0] mst_reqs;
68	  axi_resp_t [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0][Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0] mst_resps;
69	
70	  for (genvar i = 0; i < Cfg.NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_slv_port_demux
71	    logic [MstIdxWidth-1:0] dec_aw,        dec_ar;
72	    mst_port_idx_t          slv_aw_select, slv_ar_select;
73	    logic                   dec_aw_error;
74	    logic                   dec_ar_error;
75	
76	    full_req_t  decerr_req;
77	    full_resp_t decerr_resp;
78	
79	    addr_decode #(
80	      .NoIndices  ( Cfg.NoMstPorts  ),
81	      .NoRules    ( Cfg.NoAddrRules ),
82	      .addr_t     ( addr_t          ),
83	      .rule_t     ( rule_t          )
84	    ) i_axi_aw_decode (
85	      .addr_i           ( slv_ports_req_i[i].aw.addr ),
86	      .addr_map_i       ( addr_map_i                 ),
87	      .idx_o            ( dec_aw                     ),
88	      .dec_valid_o      ( /*not used*/               ),
89	      .dec_error_o      ( dec_aw_error               ),
90	      .en_default_idx_i ( en_default_mst_port_i[i]   ),
91	      .default_idx_i    ( default_mst_port_i[i]      )
92	    );
93	
94	    addr_decode #(
95	      .NoIndices  ( Cfg.NoMstPorts  ),
96	      .addr_t     ( addr_t          ),
97	      .NoRules    ( Cfg.NoAddrRules ),
98	      .rule_t     ( rule_t          )
99	    ) i_axi_ar_decode (
100	      .addr_i           ( slv_ports_req_i[i].ar.addr ),
101	      .addr_map_i       ( addr_map_i                 ),
102	      .idx_o            ( dec_ar                     ),
103	      .dec_valid_o      ( /*not used*/               ),
104	      .dec_error_o      ( dec_ar_error               ),
105	      .en_default_idx_i ( en_default_mst_port_i[i]   ),
106	      .default_idx_i    ( default_mst_port_i[i]      )
107	    );
108	
109	    assign slv_aw_select = (dec_aw_error) ?
110	        mst_port_idx_t'(Cfg.NoMstPorts) : mst_port_idx_t'(dec_aw);
111	    assign slv_ar_select = (dec_ar_error) ?
112	        mst_port_idx_t'(Cfg.NoMstPorts) : mst_port_idx_t'(dec_ar);
113	
114	    // make sure that the default slave does not get changed, if there is an unserved Ax
115	    // pragma translate_off
116	    `ifndef VERILATOR
117	    `ifndef XSIM
118	    default disable iff (~rst_ni);
119	    default_aw_mst_port_en: assert property(
120	      @(posedge clk_i) (slv_ports_req_i[i].aw_valid && !slv_ports_resp_o[i].aw_ready)
121	          |=> $stable(en_default_mst_port_i[i]))
122	        else $fatal (1, $sformatf("It is not allowed to change the default mst port\
123	                                   enable, when there is an unserved Aw beat. Slave Port: %0d", i));
124	    default_aw_mst_port: assert property(
125	      @(posedge clk_i) (slv_ports_req_i[i].aw_valid && !slv_ports_resp_o[i].aw_ready)
126	          |=> $stable(default_mst_port_i[i]))
127	        else $fatal (1, $sformatf("It is not allowed to change the default mst port\
128	                                   when there is an unserved Aw beat. Slave Port: %0d", i));
129	    default_ar_mst_port_en: assert property(
130	      @(posedge clk_i) (slv_ports_req_i[i].ar_valid && !slv_ports_resp_o[i].ar_ready)
131	          |=> $stable(en_default_mst_port_i[i]))
132	        else $fatal (1, $sformatf("It is not allowed to change the enable, when\
133	                                   there is an unserved Ar beat. Slave Port: %0d", i));
134	    default_ar_mst_port: assert property(
135	      @(posedge clk_i) (slv_ports_req_i[i].ar_valid && !slv_ports_resp_o[i].ar_ready)
136	          |=> $stable(default_mst_port_i[i]))
137	        else $fatal (1, $sformatf("It is not allowed to change the default mst port\
138	                                   when there is an unserved Ar beat. Slave Port: %0d", i));
139	    `endif
140	    `endif
141	    // pragma translate_on
142	    axi_lite_demux #(
143	      .aw_chan_t      ( aw_chan_t          ),  // AW Channel Type
144	      .w_chan_t       (  w_chan_t          ),  //  W Channel Type
145	      .b_chan_t       (  b_chan_t          ),  //  B Channel Type
146	      .ar_chan_t      ( ar_chan_t          ),  // AR Channel Type
147	      .r_chan_t       (  r_chan_t          ),  //  R Channel Type
148	      .axi_req_t      ( axi_req_t          ),
149	      .axi_resp_t     ( axi_resp_t         ),
150	      .NoMstPorts     ( Cfg.NoMstPorts + 1 ),
151	      .MaxTrans       ( Cfg.MaxMstTrans    ),
152	      .FallThrough    ( Cfg.FallThrough    ),
153	      .SpillAw        ( Cfg.LatencyMode[9] ),
154	      .SpillW         ( Cfg.LatencyMode[8] ),
155	      .SpillB         ( Cfg.LatencyMode[7] ),
156	      .SpillAr        ( Cfg.LatencyMode[6] ),
157	      .SpillR         ( Cfg.LatencyMode[5] )
158	    ) i_axi_lite_demux (
159	      .clk_i,   // Clock
160	      .rst_ni,  // Asynchronous reset active low
161	      .test_i,  // Testmode enable
162	      .slv_req_i       ( slv_ports_req_i[i]  ),
163	      .slv_aw_select_i ( slv_aw_select       ),
164	      .slv_ar_select_i ( slv_ar_select       ),
165	      .slv_resp_o      ( slv_ports_resp_o[i] ),
166	      .mst_reqs_o      ( slv_reqs[i]         ),
167	      .mst_resps_i     ( slv_resps[i]        )
168	    );
169	
170	    // connect the decode error module to the last index of the demux master port
171	    // typedef as the decode error slave uses full axi
172	    axi_lite_to_axi #(
173	      .AxiDataWidth ( Cfg.AxiDataWidth  ),
174	      .req_lite_t   ( axi_req_t         ),
175	      .resp_lite_t  ( axi_resp_t        ),
176	      .axi_req_t    ( full_req_t        ),
177	      .axi_resp_t   ( full_resp_t       )
178	    ) i_dec_err_conv (
179	      .slv_req_lite_i  ( slv_reqs[i][Cfg.NoMstPorts]  ),
180	      .slv_resp_lite_o ( slv_resps[i][Cfg.NoMstPorts] ),
181	      .slv_aw_cache_i  ( 4'd0                         ),
182	      .slv_ar_cache_i  ( 4'd0                         ),
183	      .mst_req_o       ( decerr_req                   ),
184	      .mst_resp_i      ( decerr_resp                  )
185	    );
186	
187	    axi_err_slv #(
188	      .AxiIdWidth  ( 32'd1                ), // ID width is one as defined as logic above
189	      .axi_req_t   ( full_req_t           ), // AXI request struct
190	      .axi_resp_t  ( full_resp_t          ), // AXI response struct
191	      .Resp        ( axi_pkg::RESP_DECERR ),
192	      .ATOPs       ( 1'b0                 ), // no ATOPs in AXI4-Lite
193	      .MaxTrans    ( 1                    )  // Transactions terminate at this slave, and AXI4-Lite
194	                                             // transactions have only a single beat.
195	    ) i_axi_err_slv (
196	      .clk_i      ( clk_i       ),  // Clock
197	      .rst_ni     ( rst_ni      ),  // Asynchronous reset active low
198	      .test_i     ( test_i      ),  // Testmode enable
199	      // slave port
200	      .slv_req_i  ( decerr_req  ),
201	      .slv_resp_o ( decerr_resp )
202	    );
203	  end
204	
205	  // cross all channels
206	  for (genvar i = 0; i < Cfg.NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_xbar_slv_cross
207	    for (genvar j = 0; j < Cfg.NoMstPorts; j++) begin : gen_xbar_mst_cross
208	      assign mst_reqs[j][i]  = slv_reqs[i][j];
209	      assign slv_resps[i][j] = mst_resps[j][i];
210	    end
211	  end
212	
213	  for (genvar i = 0; i < Cfg.NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_mst_port_mux
214	    axi_lite_mux #(
215	      .aw_chan_t   ( aw_chan_t          ), // AW Channel Type
216	      .w_chan_t    (  w_chan_t          ), //  W Channel Type
217	      .b_chan_t    (  b_chan_t          ), //  B Channel Type
218	      .ar_chan_t   ( ar_chan_t          ), // AR Channel Type
219	      .r_chan_t    (  r_chan_t          ), //  R Channel Type
220	      .axi_req_t   ( axi_req_t          ),
221	      .axi_resp_t  ( axi_resp_t         ),
222	      .NoSlvPorts  ( Cfg.NoSlvPorts     ), // Number of Masters for the module
223	      .MaxTrans    ( Cfg.MaxSlvTrans    ),
224	      .FallThrough ( Cfg.FallThrough    ),
225	      .SpillAw     ( Cfg.LatencyMode[4] ),
226	      .SpillW      ( Cfg.LatencyMode[3] ),
227	      .SpillB      ( Cfg.LatencyMode[2] ),
228	      .SpillAr     ( Cfg.LatencyMode[1] ),
229	      .SpillR      ( Cfg.LatencyMode[0] )
230	    ) i_axi_lite_mux (
231	      .clk_i,  // Clock
232	      .rst_ni, // Asynchronous reset active low
233	      .test_i, // Test Mode enable
234	      .slv_reqs_i  ( mst_reqs[i]         ),
235	      .slv_resps_o ( mst_resps[i]        ),
236	      .mst_req_o   ( mst_ports_req_o[i]  ),
237	      .mst_resp_i  ( mst_ports_resp_i[i] )
238	    );
239	  end
240	endmodule
241	
242	`include "axi/assign.svh"
243	
244	module axi_lite_xbar_intf #(
245	  parameter axi_pkg::xbar_cfg_t Cfg = '0,
246	  parameter type rule_t             = axi_pkg::xbar_rule_64_t
247	) (
248	  input  logic                                                    clk_i,
249	  input  logic                                                    rst_ni,
250	  input  logic                                                    test_i,
251	  AXI_LITE.Slave                                                  slv_ports [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0],
252	  AXI_LITE.Master                                                 mst_ports [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0],
253	  input  rule_t [Cfg.NoAddrRules-1:0]                             addr_map_i,
254	  input  logic  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]                              en_default_mst_port_i,
255	  input  logic  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0][$clog2(Cfg.NoMstPorts)-1:0]  default_mst_port_i
256	);
257	
258	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiAddrWidth       -1:0] addr_t;
259	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiDataWidth       -1:0] data_t;
260	  typedef logic [Cfg.AxiDataWidth/8     -1:0] strb_t;
261	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_AW_CHAN_T(aw_chan_t, addr_t)
262	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_W_CHAN_T(w_chan_t, data_t, strb_t)
263	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_B_CHAN_T(b_chan_t)
264	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_AR_CHAN_T(ar_chan_t, addr_t)
265	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_R_CHAN_T(r_chan_t, data_t)
266	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_REQ_T(axi_req_t, aw_chan_t, w_chan_t, ar_chan_t)
267	  `AXI_LITE_TYPEDEF_RESP_T(axi_resp_t, b_chan_t, r_chan_t)
268	
269	  axi_req_t   [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0]  mst_reqs;
270	  axi_resp_t  [Cfg.NoMstPorts-1:0]  mst_resps;
271	  axi_req_t   [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]  slv_reqs;
272	  axi_resp_t  [Cfg.NoSlvPorts-1:0]  slv_resps;
273	
274	  for (genvar i = 0; i < Cfg.NoMstPorts; i++) begin : gen_assign_mst
275	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_FROM_REQ(mst_ports[i], mst_reqs[i])
276	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_TO_RESP(mst_resps[i], mst_ports[i])
277	  end
278	
279	  for (genvar i = 0; i < Cfg.NoSlvPorts; i++) begin : gen_assign_slv
280	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_TO_REQ(slv_reqs[i], slv_ports[i])
281	    `AXI_LITE_ASSIGN_FROM_RESP(slv_ports[i], slv_resps[i])
282	  end
283	
284	  axi_lite_xbar #(
285	    .Cfg  (Cfg),
286	    .aw_chan_t  ( aw_chan_t  ),
287	    .w_chan_t   ( w_chan_t   ),
288	    .b_chan_t   ( b_chan_t   ),
289	    .ar_chan_t  ( ar_chan_t  ),
290	    .r_chan_t   ( r_chan_t   ),
291	    .axi_req_t  ( axi_req_t  ),
292	    .axi_resp_t ( axi_resp_t ),
293	    .rule_t     ( rule_t     )
294	  ) i_xbar (
295	    .clk_i,
296	    .rst_ni,
297	    .test_i,
298	    .slv_ports_req_i  (slv_reqs ),
299	    .slv_ports_resp_o (slv_resps),
300	    .mst_ports_req_o  (mst_reqs ),
301	    .mst_ports_resp_i (mst_resps),
302	    .addr_map_i,
303	    .en_default_mst_port_i,
304	    .default_mst_port_i
305	  );
306	
307	endmodule

1	# AXI4-Lite Fully-Connected Crossbar
2	
3	`axi_lite_xbar` is a fully-connected crossbar that implements the AXI4-Lite specification.
4	
5	## Design Overview
6	
7	`axi_lite_xbar` is a fully-connected crossbar, which means that each master module that is connected to a *slave port* for of the crossbar has direct wires to all slave modules that are connected to the *master ports* of the crossbar.
8	A block-diagram of the crossbar is shown below:
9	
10	![Block-diagram showing the design of the full AXI4-Lite Crossbar.](axi_lite_xbar.png  "Block-diagram showing the design of the full AXI4-Lite Crossbar.")
11	
12	The crossbar has a configurable number of slave and master ports.
13	
14	## Address Map
15	
16	`axi_lite_xbar` uses the [same address decoding scheme](axi_xbar.md#address-map) as `axi_xbar`.
17	
18	## Decode Errors and Default Slave Port
19	
20	Each slave port has its own internal *decode error slave* module.  If the address of a transaction does not match any rule, the transaction is routed to that decode error slave module.  That module absorbs each transaction and responds with a decode error (with the proper number of beats).  The data of each read response beat is `32'hBADCAB1E` (zero-extended or truncated to match the data width).
21	
22	Each slave port can have a default master port.  If the default master port is enabled for a slave port, any address on that slave port that does not match any rule is routed to the default master port instead of the decode error slave.  The default master port can be enabled and changed at run time (it is an input signal to the crossbar), and the same restrictions as for the address map apply.
23	
24	
25	## Configuration
26	
27	The crossbar is configured through the `Cfg` parameter with a `axi_pkg::xbar_cfg_t` struct.  That struct has the following fields:
28	
29	| Name                 | Type               | Definition                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
30	|:---------------------|:-------------------|:------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
31	| `NoSlvPorts`         | `int unsigned`     | The number of AXI4-Lite slave ports of the crossbar (in other words, how many AXI4-Lite master modules can be attached).                                                                                                                                          |
32	| `NoMstPorts`         | `int unsigned`     | The number of AXI4-Lite master ports of the crossbar (in other words, how many AXI4-Lite slave modules can be attached).                                                                                                                                          |
33	| `MaxMstTrans`        | `int unsigned`     | Each slave port can have at most this many transactions [in flight](../doc#in-flight).                                                                                                                                                                            |
34	| `MaxSlvTrans`        | `int unsigned`     | Each master port can have at most this many transactions [in flight](../doc#in-flight).                                                                                                                                                                           |
35	| `FallThrough`        | `bit`              | Routing decisions on the AW channel fall through to the W channel.  Enabling this allows the crossbar to accept a W beat in the same cycle as the corresponding AW beat, but it increases the combinatorial path of the W channel with logic from the AW channel. |
36	| `LatencyMode`        | `enum logic [9:0]` | Latency on the individual channels, defined in detail in section *Pipelining and Latency* below.                                                                                                                                                                  |
37	| `AxiIdWidthSlvPorts` | `int unsigned`     | Not used by the AXI4-Lite crossbar. Set `default: '0`.                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
38	| `AxiIdUsedSlvPorts`  | `int unsigned`     | Not used by the AXI4-Lite crossbar. Set `default: '0`.                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
39	| `AxiAddrWidth`       | `int unsigned`     | The AXI4-Lite address width.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
40	| `AxiDataWidth`       | `int unsigned`     | The AXI4-Lite data width.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
41	| `NoAddrRules`        | `int unsigned`     | The number of address map rules.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
42	
43	The other parameters are types to define the ports of the crossbar.  The `*_chan_t` and `*_req_t`/`*_resp_t` types must be bound in accordance to the configuration with the `AXI_TYPEDEF` macros defined in `axi/typedef.svh`.  The `rule_t` type must be bound to an address decoding rule with the same address width as in the configuration, and `axi_pkg` contains definitions for 64- and 32-bit addresses.
44	
45	### Pipelining and Latency
46	
47	The `LatencyMode` parameter allows to insert spill registers after each channel (AW, W, B, AR, and R) of each master port (i.e., each multiplexer) and before each channel of each slave port (i.e., each demultiplexer).  Spill registers cut combinatorial paths, so this parameter reduces the length of combinatorial paths through the crossbar.
48	
49	Some common configurations are given in the `xbar_latency_e` `enum`.  The recommended configuration (`CUT_ALL_AX`) is to have a latency of 2 on the AW and AR channels because these channels have the most combinatorial logic on them.  Additionally, `FallThrough` should be set to `0` to prevent logic on the AW channel from extending combinatorial paths on the W channel.  However, it is possible to run the crossbar in a fully combinatorial configuration by setting `LatencyMode` to `NO_LATENCY` and `FallThrough` to `1`.
50	If two crossbars are connected in both directions, meaning both have one of their master_ports connected to a slave_port of the other, it is required to have either `CUT_SLV_PORTS`, `CUT_MST_PORTS` or `CUT_ALL_PORTS` as the configuration of the two crossbars. This is to prevent timing loops. The other configurations will lead to timing loops in simulation and synthesis on the not cut channels between the two crossbars.
51	
52	## Ports
53	
54	| Name                    | Description                                                                                                                                                                   |
55	|:------------------------|:------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
56	| `clk_i`                 | Clock to which all other signals (except `rst_ni`) are synchronous.                                                                                                           |
57	| `rst_ni`                | Reset, asynchronous, active-low.                                                                                                                                              |
58	| `test_i`                | Test mode enable (active-high).                                                                                                                                               |
59	| `slv_ports_*`           | Array of slave ports of the crossbar.  The array index of each port is the index of the slave port.  This index will be prepended to all requests at one of the master ports. |
60	| `mst_ports_*`           | Array of master ports of the crossbar.  The array index of each port is the index of the master port.                                                                         |
61	| `addr_map_i`            | Address map of the crossbar (see section *Address Map* above).                                                                                                                |
62	| `en_default_mst_port_i` | One bit per slave port that defines whether the default master port is active for that slave port (see section *Decode Errors and Default Slave Port* above).                 |
63	| `default_mst_port_i`    | One master port index per slave port that defines the default master port for that slave port (if active).                                                                    |
64	
65	
66	## Ordering and Stalls
67	
68	The ordering inside the crossbar is organized by a network of FIFO's ensuring the in order transaction transmission. It is possible to have multiple transactions to different master ports in flight, however when one of the slave modules connected to these master ports stalls, other consecutive in fight transaction will also be stalled.
69	
70	## Verification Methodology
71	
72	This module has been verified with a directed random verification testbench, described and implemented in `test/tb_axi_lite_xbar.sv`.
73	
74	
75	## Design Rationale for No Pipelining Inside Crossbar
76	
77	This is identical to the issue described in [`axi_xbar`](axi_xbar.md#design-rationale-for-no-pipelining-inside-crossbar).

file.content:
# AXI4-Lite Fully-Connected Crossbar

`axi_lite_xbar` is a fully-connected crossbar that implements the AXI4-Lite specification.

## Design Overview

`axi_lite_xbar` is a fully-connected crossbar, which means that each master module that is connected to a *slave port* for of the crossbar has direct wires to all slave modules that are connected to the *master ports* of the crossbar.
A block-diagram of the crossbar is shown below:

![Block-diagram showing the design of the full AXI4-Lite Crossbar.](axi_lite_xbar.png  "Block-diagram showing the design of the full AXI4-Lite Crossbar.")

The crossbar has a configurable number of slave and master ports.

## Address Map

`axi_lite_xbar` uses the [same address decoding scheme](axi_xbar.md#address-map) as `axi_xbar`.

## Decode Errors and Default Slave Port

Each slave port has its own internal *decode error slave* module.  If the address of a transaction does not match any rule, the transaction is routed to that decode error slave module.  That module absorbs each transaction and responds with a decode error (with the proper number of beats).  The data of each read response beat is `32'hBADCAB1E` (zero-extended or truncated to match the data width).

Each slave port can have a default master port.  If the default master port is enabled for a slave port, any address on that slave port that does not match any rule is routed to the default master port instead of the decode error slave.  The default master port can be enabled and changed at run time (it is an input signal to the crossbar), and the same restrictions as for the address map apply.


## Configuration

The crossbar is configured through the `Cfg` parameter with a `axi_pkg::xbar_cfg_t` struct.  That struct has the following fields:

| Name                 | Type               | Definition                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|:---------------------|:-------------------|:------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `NoSlvPorts`         | `int unsigned`     | The number of AXI4-Lite slave ports of the crossbar (in other words, how many AXI4-Lite master modules can be attached).                                                                                                                                          |
| `NoMstPorts`         | `int unsigned`     | The number of AXI4-Lite master ports of the crossbar (in other words, how many AXI4-Lite slave modules can be attached).                                                                                                                                          |
| `MaxMstTrans`        | `int unsigned`     | Each slave port can have at most this many transactions [in flight](../doc#in-flight).                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `MaxSlvTrans`        | `int unsigned`     | Each master port can have at most this many transactions [in flight](../doc#in-flight).                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `FallThrough`        | `bit`              | Routing decisions on the AW channel fall through to the W channel.  Enabling this allows the crossbar to accept a W beat in the same cycle as the corresponding AW beat, but it increases the combinatorial path of the W channel with logic from the AW channel. |
| `LatencyMode`        | `enum logic [9:0]` | Latency on the individual channels, defined in detail in section *Pipelining and Latency* below.                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `AxiIdWidthSlvPorts` | `int unsigned`     | Not used by the AXI4-Lite crossbar. Set `default: '0`.                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `AxiIdUsedSlvPorts`  | `int unsigned`     | Not used by the AXI4-Lite crossbar. Set `default: '0`.                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `AxiAddrWidth`       | `int unsigned`     | The AXI4-Lite address width.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `AxiDataWidth`       | `int unsigned`     | The AXI4-Lite data width.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `NoAddrRules`        | `int unsigned`     | The number of address map rules.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

The other parameters are types to define the ports of the crossbar.  The `*_chan_t` and `*_req_t`/`*_resp_t` types must be bound in accordance to the configuration with the `AXI_TYPEDEF` macros defined in `axi/typedef.svh`.  The `rule_t` type must be bound to an address decoding rule with the same address width as in the configuration, and `axi_pkg` contains definitions for 64- and 32-bit addresses.

### Pipelining and Latency

The `LatencyMode` parameter allows to insert spill registers after each channel (AW, W, B, AR, and R) of each master port (i.e., each multiplexer) and before each channel of each slave port (i.e., each demultiplexer).  Spill registers cut combinatorial paths, so this parameter reduces the length of combinatorial paths through the crossbar.

Some common configurations are given in the `xbar_latency_e` `enum`.  The recommended configuration (`CUT_ALL_AX`) is to have a latency of 2 on the AW and AR channels because these channels have the most combinatorial logic on them.  Additionally, `FallThrough` should be set to `0` to prevent logic on the AW channel from extending combinatorial paths on the W channel.  However, it is possible to run the crossbar in a fully combinatorial configuration by setting `LatencyMode` to `NO_LATENCY` and `FallThrough` to `1`.
If two crossbars are connected in both directions, meaning both have one of their master_ports connected to a slave_port of the other, it is required to have either `CUT_SLV_PORTS`, `CUT_MST_PORTS` or `CUT_ALL_PORTS` as the configuration of the two crossbars. This is to prevent timing loops. The other configurations will lead to timing loops in simulation and synthesis on the not cut channels between the two crossbars.

## Ports

| Name                    | Description                                                                                                                                                                   |
|:------------------------|:------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `clk_i`                 | Clock to which all other signals (except `rst_ni`) are synchronous.                                                                                                           |
| `rst_ni`                | Reset, asynchronous, active-low.                                                                                                                                              |
| `test_i`                | Test mode enable (active-high).                                                                                                                                               |
| `slv_ports_*`           | Array of slave ports of the crossbar.  The array index of each port is the index of the slave port.  This index will be prepended to all requests at one of the master ports. |
| `mst_ports_*`           | Array of master ports of the crossbar.  The array index of each port is the index of the master port.                                                                         |
| `addr_map_i`            | Address map of the crossbar (see section *Address Map* above).                                                                                                                |
| `en_default_mst_port_i` | One bit per slave port that defines whether the default master port is active for that slave port (see section *Decode Errors and Default Slave Port* above).                 |
| `default_mst_port_i`    | One master port index per slave port that defines the default master port for that slave port (if active).                                                                    |


## Ordering and Stalls

The ordering inside the crossbar is organized by a network of FIFO's ensuring the in order transaction transmission. It is possible to have multiple transactions to different master ports in flight, however when one of the slave modules connected to these master ports stalls, other consecutive in fight transaction will also be stalled.

## Verification Methodology

This module has been verified with a directed random verification testbench, described and implemented in `test/tb_axi_lite_xbar.sv`.


## Design Rationale for No Pipelining Inside Crossbar

This is identical to the issue described in [`axi_xbar`](axi_xbar.md#design-rationale-for-no-pipelining-inside-crossbar).
